diff:
diff --git a/.github/workflows/echo.yml b/.github/workflows/echo.yml
index b92c70c..19148f1 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/echo.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/echo.yml
@@ -40,12 +40,21 @@ jobs:
       - name: Run Tests
         run: go test -race --coverprofile=coverage.coverprofile --covermode=atomic ./...
 
-      - name: Upload coverage to Codecov
+      - name: Upload coverage to Codecov (core)
         if: success() && matrix.go == env.LATEST_GO_VERSION && matrix.os == 'ubuntu-latest'
         uses: codecov/codecov-action@v5
         with:
           token:
           fail_ci_if_error: false
+          flags: core
+
+      - name: Upload coverage to Codecov (middleware)
+        if: success() && matrix.go == env.LATEST_GO_VERSION && matrix.os == 'ubuntu-latest'
+        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v5
+        with:
+          token:
+          fail_ci_if_error: false
+          flags: middleware
 
   benchmark:
     needs: test
diff --git a/codecov.yml b/codecov.yml
index 0fa3a3f..0294237 100644
--- a/codecov.yml
+++ b/codecov.yml
@@ -3,9 +3,28 @@ coverage:
     project:
       default:
         threshold: 1%
+      core:
+        flags:
+          - core
+        threshold: 1%
+      middleware:
+        flags:
+          - middleware
+        threshold: 5%
     patch:
       default:
         threshold: 1%
 
+flag_management:
+  default_rules:
+    carryforward: true
+  individual_flags:
+    - name: core
+      paths:
+        - "*.go"
+    - name: middleware
+      paths:
+        - "middleware/*.go"
+
 comment:
   require_changes: true

exploration_log.aha_moment: Codecov's flag_management with individual_flags.paths allows path-based filtering of coverage reports. Two flags (core/middleware) map to path patterns. The workflow uploads the same report twice with different flags and codecov filters by path.
exploration_log.hindsight: Straightforward YAML config change. Read codecov.yml docs to understand flag_management schema.

alternatives: Run separate test commands per package and upload with flags
Use flag_management with path filtering and upload same report with both flags
key_decisions.reasoning: Simpler workflow - upload the same combined coverage report twice with different flags; codecov filters by path. No need to run tests separately.

search_sequence.action: Read codecov.yml to understand current configuration
search_sequence.reasoning: Need to understand the existing structure before adding flags
search_sequence.result: Simple coverage config with project and patch thresholds

search_sequence.reasoning: Need to find the coverage upload step to add flag labels
search_sequence.result: Single 'Upload coverage to Codecov' step with no flags parameter

queries.justification: Rule compliance: file paths and key names. Pre-implementation.
queries.query_text: Add codecov flags for core and middleware packages in codecov.yml with separate thresholds and update echo.yml to pass flag labels

solve_notes: Updated `codecov.yml` to add `flag_management` with `core` (path: `*.go`) and `middleware` (path: `middleware/*.go`) flags and separate `coverage.status.project` entries with distinct thresholds. Updated `.github/workflows/echo.yml` to upload coverage twice with the respective flag labels.
task_text:
The `codecov.yml` at the repo root defines global coverage settings but
does not set separate coverage targets for the core `echo` package
versus the `middleware/` subpackage. The middleware package has different
coverage characteristics — many middlewares have edge cases difficult to
cover in unit tests. Add `flags` and `coverage.status.project` entries
in `codecov.yml` that set separate thresholds for root `*.go` files and
`middleware/*.go` files. Update `.github/workflows/echo.yml` to pass
the correct coverage flag labels when uploading reports.

test_selection.coverage_skip_reason: Changed files are YAML configuration files (codecov.yml, .github/workflows/echo.yml) which are not Go source and are not covered by the Go test coverage report.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: comment
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: an AI agent would read the full existing codecov.yml to understand the current structure before adding new sections

tier_difference_reasoning: The comment key and existing coverage structure are in thrash_preventing because an AI agent would read the full codecov.yml to understand the existing YAML structure before inserting new sections, to avoid misplacing keys or duplicating existing ones.
